analyst: Goldman Sachs - Vice President, Equity Research

predicted_questions.question_text: Hi there. Good morning. Thanks for taking the question. I'm curious on two things: (1) can you decompose the 1.5%–2.5% Net Yield guide — roughly what portion (in bps or %) is coming from new‑ship and destination premiums (Legend/Icon deliveries, Royal Beach Club openings) versus like‑for‑like ticket/onboard pricing and mix? and (2) you called out Q2 Net Yields of about +0.2% and that Mediterranean and West Coast Mexico moderations were temporary and now rebounding — can you quantify, in bps, how much of the Q2/Q3 yield drag is from itinerary/geographic mix (Med/WC Mexico) versus timing effects (dry‑dock days, air/flight disruption impacts)? Thanks.
predicted_questions.rationale: This mirrors the persona's preference for a decomposition-style, two-part yield question (coverage_profile.rhetorical_signature and reasoning_style.evidence_demanded) and targets recurring_concerns.core_topics[0] (Yield composition). It anchors to management's statements that Net Yields are guided to 1.5%–2.5%, Q2 Net Yields are ~0.2% (CFO), and comments about Legend/Icon, Royal Beach Club demand and the temporary Mediterranean/West Coast of Mexico moderation (CEO/CFO).

predicted_questions.question_text: Hi there. Good morning. Thanks for taking the question. On costs and cadence: you said Net Cruise Costs ex‑fuel will be roughly flat for the year but up 4.6%–5.1% in Q2, and that Q2 carries almost 400 bps of cost headwinds from extra dry‑dock days, crew travel and year‑over‑year comparatives — can you walk through that in more detail and give a dollar or bps split across the major buckets (dry‑dock maintenance/depreciation, crew travel, other operating costs), and indicate when you expect each of those to abate through the back half of the year? Thanks.
predicted_questions.rationale: This question follows the persona's recurring focus on cost timing and dry‑dock impacts (recurring_concerns.core_topics[1]) and asks for the concrete decomposition the analyst typically demands (reasoning_style.evidence_demanded). It directly references the CFO's remarks that Net Cruise Costs ex‑fuel are expected to be up 4.6%–5.1% in Q2 and that the quarter includes almost 400 bps of dry‑dock and travel‑related headwinds.
